source: Search Retargeting using Directed Query Embeddings

concepts.finding: query2vec: Two-level hierarchical neural language model. Upper layer models temporal context of query sequences (skip-gram). Bottom layer models word sequences within a query (CBOW). Optimizes joint log-likelihood of: (1) predicting neighboring queries, (2) predicting query from its words, (3) predicting words from context+query. Trained on 12B search sessions, 45M+ query vectors.
concepts.implication: Temporal query sequences (sessions) can be leveraged to learn query embeddings that capture search intent transitions. Joint word+query modeling helps tail queries by relying more on content vs context.
concepts.open_question: How to define session boundaries for keyword clustering when search history is not available? Can synthetic sequences be generated?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Session-trained query embeddings yield better intent clustering than single-query embeddings because they capture reformulation patterns.

concepts.finding: Directed ad-query2vec modeled temporal direction (only past queries predict future) and incorporated ad click context. Achieved 0.91% yield rate improvement and 1.12% CTR improvement over standard keyword lists. Increased user coverage by 13%.
concepts.implication: Directed (causal) embeddings that only use past context can better predict future user intent. Incorporating negative signals (skipped ads) improves embedding quality for intent prediction.
concepts.open_question: Should keyword-intent clustering use undirected (symmetric) or directed (causal) co-occurrence?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Directed embeddings that only use context preceding a keyword in a session will form more predictive intent clusters than undirected embeddings.
